title: armeria villosa subsp. enritrianoi
intro: Armeria villosa subsp. enritrianoi, also known as the Enriqueta armeria or Enriqueta marsh thistle, is a subspecies of the Armeria plant, a type of flowering plant in the tea rose family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing stress and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
